evals.prompt: I want to write requirements for adding team billing to our SaaS app. We've already scoped it — the living doc is at plans/team-billing.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the living doc (which has ## Scope) and acknowledges its contents. Conducts a structured product interview covering all 9 product domains (problem confirmation, target users, user stories, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, success metrics, scope, dependencies, risks) one question at a time with recommendations. Writes ## Requirements into the living doc with user stories with acceptance criteria, functional requirements, success metrics table, and detailed scope. Then automatically runs a quality gate — delivering a Ready/Revise/Rethink verdict. If Ready, checks glossary conditions and writes ### Glossary under ## Requirements if needed. Should NOT include data models, API designs, architecture decisions, build phases, or technical implementation details. Should end by suggesting '/design' (UI features) or '/architect' (backend-only).

evals.prompt: Let's write requirements for a dashboard sharing feature. Users need to share dashboards with people outside the org via links, with control over what data is visible.
evals.expected_output: Checks for a scope document (may or may not find one). Conducts a product-focused interview exploring user personas (internal admin sharing, external viewer), user stories (create share link, view shared dashboard, revoke access), functional requirements (link generation, permission controls, data filtering from user perspective), success metrics (adoption rate, external viewer engagement), and scope boundaries. Produces a PRD focused entirely on what users see and do. Then runs quality gate and delivers verdict. Should NOT specify auth token implementation, API endpoints, or database schemas. Should end by suggesting '/design' or '/architect'.

evals.prompt: define: We're building AI-powered search to replace our keyword search. Users complain they can't find things.
evals.expected_output: Conducts a product-focused interview exploring who searches (roles, frequency, technical sophistication), what they search for (content types, query patterns), what good search results look like from the user's perspective, success metrics (search success rate, time to find), and non-functional requirements (response time, relevance quality). Produces a PRD with user stories like 'As a user, I want to search in natural language so that I find relevant docs without knowing exact keywords.' Then runs quality gate. Should NOT specify embedding models, vector databases, indexing strategies, or retrieval algorithms. Should end by suggesting '/design' or '/architect'.

evals.prompt: review the requirements in plans/team-billing.md — are they ready for technical design?
evals.expected_output: Detects existing ## Requirements in the living doc and skips interview, going directly to quality gate. Delivers a structured verdict (Ready/Revise/Rethink) with strengths, issues grounded in product analysis and codebase verification, risks, and next step. Should check ## Scope section and verify alignment. Should NOT re-interview the user during analysis. If Revise, should enter Resolution Phase — walking through issue-groups with suggested resolutions, updating ## Requirements. If Ready, checks glossary conditions. Should end by suggesting '/design' or '/architect'.

evals.prompt: define: The auth flows requirements in plans/auth-flows.md need a review. I suspect the acceptance criteria are vague and the success metrics aren't measurable.
evals.expected_output: Detects existing ## Requirements in the living doc and goes to quality gate. Delivers a Revise verdict with numbered issues. Transitions into Resolution Phase: groups related issues by section, presents concrete suggested resolutions, asks user to accept/modify/skip each group. After all groups addressed, updates ## Requirements. Re-runs quality gate on updated requirements. Should batch aggressively if user accepts without modification.

evals.prompt: define: Look at plans/gamification.md. The feature is about adding a points and badges system but I'm not sure the problem statement even makes sense for our B2B product.
evals.expected_output: Detects existing ## Requirements in the living doc and goes to quality gate. Likely delivers a Rethink verdict due to fundamental problems (unclear problem-market fit for B2B). Should NOT enter Resolution Phase on Rethink. Should recommend rolling back to /explore to re-scope. Should explain why patching individual issues won't fix fundamentally misaligned requirements.

evals.prompt: I just finished the requirements for team billing. Can you define the domain terms before we start the technical design? The living doc is plans/team-billing.md
evals.expected_output: Detects existing ## Requirements in the living doc, runs quality gate (should be Ready if well-written). Then checks glossary conditions — team billing likely introduces domain nouns (subscription, plan, seat, invoice). Writes ### Glossary under ## Requirements with terms, definitions, aliases, code names, and status. Should flag ambiguities like 'plan' (billing plan vs project plan). Should NOT modify the requirements content above the glossary. Should end by suggesting '/architect'.

evals.prompt: define: We've scoped team billing already (plans/team-billing.md has ## Scope). Let's write the requirements.
evals.expected_output: Reads the living doc's ## Scope section. Skips Problem & motivation domain entirely (already resolved in scope). Opens interview at Target users & personas (domain 2). Should NOT re-ask about what the problem is, why now, or what happens if we don't build it. Should reference scope findings naturally: 'The scope identifies [stakeholders]. Let's define the target user personas more precisely.'

evals.prompt: define: The architect skill hit a requirements gap. The living doc plans/team-billing.md has ## Rollback Notes saying the billing model assumption (per-seat vs per-org) wasn't resolved in requirements.
evals.expected_output: Reads ## Rollback Notes for trigger, affected domains, and decisions to preserve. Reads existing ## Requirements. Resumes interview ONLY for the affected domain (functional requirements around billing model) — does NOT re-interview target users, user stories, or other resolved domains. After resolving the billing model question, updates ## Requirements and re-runs quality gate. Clears ## Rollback Notes after resolving.
